issues.title: Daily Log page lacks loading, error, and empty states
issues.description: The Daily Log page renders mock data inline with no skeleton loader on mount, no error boundary for failed fetches, and no contextual empty state when no logs exist. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §7 items 11, 12, and 13 all violated. Users see either raw mock rows or a blank table.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/DailyLog.tsx, in the default exported component function, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The component renders a table of daily logs directly from hardcoded mock data with no loading, error, or empty state handling. There is no isLoading guard, no error display, and when the data array is empty the table just renders with zero rows. AFTER: (1) Add a `const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true)` and a `const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null)` state. In a useEffect, simulate data fetch by wrapping the mock data assignment in a setTimeout(300ms) that sets isLoading to false, or if using a real hook set states from it. (2) When isLoading is true, render a skeleton loader: 5 rows of pulsing gray rectangles (height 48px, borderRadius 8px, background '#E5E7EB', animation pulse) matching the table column layout. (3) When error is not null, render an inline error banner with red background, the error message text, and a 'Retry' button that calls the fetch function again. (4) When data array is empty and not loading, render a contextual empty state: a Calendar icon (from lucide-react, size 48, color '#9CA3AF'), heading 'No daily logs yet', subtext 'The daily log is your project official record. Start documenting site conditions today.', and a primary orange button 'Start Today Log'. Add aria-live='polite' to the container wrapping these states so screen readers announce transitions. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Safety page has no responsive layout for mobile viewports
issues.description: The Safety page uses fixed-width grid layouts and desktop-sized tables with no breakpoint handling. At 375px width, metric cards overflow horizontally, table columns overlap, and touch targets on action buttons are under 44px. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §11 item 11 requires mobile-optimized incident reporting. Global standard requires usability at 375px.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Safety.tsx, in the default exported component function, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The metric cards grid uses `gridTemplateColumns: 'repeat(4, 1fr)'` or similar fixed multi-column layout with no media query or responsive adjustment. Table rows have small action buttons under 30px height. AFTER: (1) Change the metric cards container to use `gridTemplateColumns: 'repeat(auto-fit, minmax(240px, 1fr))'` so cards stack to 1-2 columns on narrow screens. (2) Add `overflowX: 'auto'` and `WebkitOverflowScrolling: 'touch'` to the table wrapper div so tables scroll horizontally on mobile. (3) Ensure all action buttons (edit, view, create incident) have `minHeight: '44px'` and `minWidth: '44px'` for touch targets per Apple HIG. (4) Set page container padding to `padding: window.innerWidth < 768 ? '16px' : '32px'` using a simple CSS approach: add a style block or use the existing theme spacing with a conditional. Prefer `padding: spacing.md` at the page level and let the grid handle responsive wrapping. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Field Capture page missing ARIA labels on interactive photo grid elements
issues.description: The Field Capture page renders a photo grid where each photo card is clickable but has no role='button', no aria-label, and no keyboard focus handling. The capture button lacks an accessible name. Screen readers cannot navigate or activate any element. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §14 requires camera-first UX but Global Standards require all interactive elements to be keyboard navigable with ARIA labels.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/FieldCapture.tsx, in the default exported component function, make these specific changes: BEFORE: Photo grid items are div elements with onClick handlers but no role, tabIndex, aria-label, or onKeyDown. The main 'Capture' button may lack an aria-label describing its action. AFTER: (1) On each clickable photo card div, add `role='button'`, `tabIndex={0}`, `aria-label={`View photo: ${photo.title || photo.id}`}`, and `onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); handlePhotoClick(photo); } }}`. (2) On the main capture/camera button, add `aria-label='Capture new field photo'`. (3) On any filter dropdown or toggle, add appropriate `aria-label` attributes describing their purpose (e.g., `aria-label='Filter photos by category'`). (4) Wrap the photo grid in a `<section aria-label='Field capture photos'>` element. (5) Add `aria-live='polite'` to the container that shows photo count or status messages so screen readers announce updates. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Safety page missing OSHA TRIR calculation and Days Since Last Incident counter
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §11 items 2 and 3 require a prominent 'Days Since Last Incident' counter and auto-calculated TRIR metric. The current Safety page shows placeholder or static metric values instead of computing these from incident data. TRIR formula: (Total Recordable Incidents × 200,000) / Total Hours Worked. Days counter resets on medical_treatment+ severity incidents only.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Safety.tsx, in the default exported component function, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The metric cards show hardcoded or static values for safety KPIs. There is no computation of TRIR or days-since-last-incident from the incident data array. AFTER: (1) From the incidents data array (mock or real), compute `daysSinceLastIncident`: find the most recent incident with severity in ['medical_treatment', 'lost_time', 'fatality'], calculate `Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(lastIncident.date).getTime()) / 86400000)`. If no such incident exists, show 'No recordable incidents'. (2) Compute TRIR: count incidents where severity is in ['medical_treatment', 'lost_time', 'fatality'], then calculate `(count * 200000) / totalHoursWorked`. Use a reasonable totalHoursWorked value from crew data or default to 50000 if not available. Display as a number with 2 decimal places. (3) Display 'Days Since Last Incident' as the first and most prominent metric card with large font (28px bold), green color if > 30 days, amber if 10-30, red if < 10. (4) Display TRIR as a second metric card with industry benchmark comparison text below: 'Industry avg: 2.8'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Daily Log form missing 'Same as yesterday' quick-fill and default toggles
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA §7 items 16 and 17 require a 'Same as yesterday' button that pre-fills crew and equipment from the prior log, and default-ON toggles for 'No incidents today' and 'No visitors today' to collapse those sections. Neither exists. This directly impacts the 90-second submission target (item 15).
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/DailyLog.tsx, in the component that renders the daily log creation or edit form, make these specific changes: BEFORE: The form shows all sections expanded with empty fields. There is no mechanism to copy data from the previous day's log. Incident and visitor sections are always expanded requiring manual input even when empty. AFTER: (1) Add a button at the top of the form labeled 'Same as Yesterday' with an icon (Copy from lucide-react). On click, it reads the most recent daily log entry from the data array and pre-fills the crew entries, equipment entries, and visitor list into the current form state. Show a toast 'Copied from previous log' on success. (2) Add two toggle switches at the top of the Incidents and Visitors sections respectively: 'No incidents today' (default: checked/ON) and 'No visitors today' (default: checked/ON). When toggled ON, collapse/hide the respective section content. When toggled OFF, expand and show the input fields. Use a simple `useState<boolean>(true)` for each toggle. Style toggles with the orange primary color when active. (3) Ensure the 'Same as Yesterday' button has `aria-label='Copy crew and equipment from previous daily log'`. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: Fieldwire leads in field-first UX with true offline photo capture, Apple Pencil markup, and two-step punch verification. Procore Quick Capture converts video to punch items with automatic transcription. SiteSync field-operations currently has none of these capabilities functioning: no real offline queue, no voice-to-text, no auto-weather population, no OSHA-compliant incident tracking with computed KPIs. The Safety page lacks TRIR and incident rate calculations that every safety manager expects. The Daily Log misses the 90-second submission target by a wide margin due to missing auto-fill and section defaulting. Opusense AI offers voice-to-text field reports that auto-organize with photos, a feature SiteSync claims but has not implemented.
summary: Field Operations scores 22/100, a 4-point regression from the prior cycle. The module covers Daily Log, Field Capture, and Safety pages, all of which fail critical PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA checks. Daily Log has no loading/error/empty states, no 'Same as yesterday' quick-fill, and no default toggles for incident and visitor sections, making the 90-second submission target unreachable. Safety lacks computed OSHA metrics (TRIR, Days Since Last Incident) and has no mobile-responsive layout, rendering it unusable on field devices. Field Capture photo grid elements are entirely inaccessible to keyboard and screen reader users. The five surgical fixes target: (1) Daily Log loading/error/empty states, (2) Safety mobile responsiveness, (3) Field Capture ARIA accessibility, (4) Safety OSHA KPI calculations, and (5) Daily Log quick-fill and default toggles. These fixes address the most impactful violations without adding new features.
